name: collection_info
description:
Get the structure and summary statistics of the Anki collection. Returns available note types with their field names, deck names with note counts, all tags in use, and scheduling statistics (cards due, new cards, per-deck summaries). Use this to orient yourself before creating or searching for notes — especially to discover which note types, fields, and decks exist.

With no arguments, returns a compact summary (counts, dates, collection path). Use `include` to request specific sections, or "all" for everything. Note type summaries include field names and type (standard/cloze) but not full template HTML or CSS — use `note_type_details` to request full definitions for specific note types when you need to inspect or author templates.

include.description: Sections to return. Any combination of "summary" (counts, dates, path), "note_types" (note types and their fields), "decks" (deck hierarchy with note counts), "tags" (all tags in use), "stats" (card counts, due counts, per-deck summaries), or "all" for everything. Defaults to ["summary"].

note_type_details.description: List of note type names to return full definitions for, including card template HTML and CSS styling (e.g., ["Basic", "Japanese Vocabulary"]). Omit to return only summaries (field names and type).

name: list_notes
description:
Retrieve notes matching structured filters — by deck, tags, note type, note IDs, or modification date. Returns note metadata and field content.

Use this for precise lookups: fetching specific notes by ID, listing a deck's contents, or filtering by exact criteria. For conceptual or fuzzy queries ("cards about mitochondrial membrane potential"), use search_notes instead.

At least one filter must be provided. Combine filters freely — they are ANDed together. Use `fields: "meta"` to return only metadata for large result sets. The response includes `total` (full match count); if more notes matched than `limit` allows, narrow your filters rather than attempting to retrieve all of them.

deck.description: Filter to notes in this deck. Use "::" for nested decks (e.g., "Japanese::Vocabulary"). Includes child decks.

tags.description: Filter to notes having all of these tags. Prefix with "-" to exclude (e.g., ["-leech", "verb"] matches notes tagged "verb" but not "leech").

note_type.description: Filter to notes using this note type (e.g., "Basic", "Cloze").

modified_since.description: ISO 8601 date or datetime. Only return notes modified after this time (e.g., "2026-05-01" or "2026-05-01T14:00:00Z").

query.description: Raw Anki search query for advanced filtering (e.g., "is:due", "prop:ivl>=30", "rated:7"). Combined with other filters via AND. Syntax: https://docs.ankiweb.net/searching.html

fields.description: "full" (default) returns all field content. "meta" returns only note ID, note type, deck, tags, and modification time — useful for large result sets.

name: search_notes
description:
Semantic similarity search over the Anki collection. Accepts natural-language query strings, note IDs (to find conceptually similar notes), or both. Returns the top matches ranked by similarity score.

Use this for conceptual queries that keyword search cannot handle: finding cards about a topic, checking if a concept is already covered before creating new cards, identifying related or near-duplicate notes, or exploring thematic clusters in the collection. Read the returned content and reason about overlap in natural language — don't rely on numeric scores for decision-making.

At least one of `queries` or `ids` must be provided. Each query string and each source ID produces its own ranked result list.

queries.description: Natural-language search strings. Each is independently matched against the collection (e.g., ["electron transport chain regulation", "citric acid cycle inhibitors"]).

ids.description: Note IDs to use as search anchors — returns notes semantically similar to these existing notes. Source notes are automatically excluded from results.

top_k.description: Maximum results per query or source ID. Default 10.

deck.description: Restrict search to notes in this deck (includes child decks).

tags.description: Restrict search to notes matching all of these tags.

exclude_ids.description: Additional note IDs to exclude from results (source notes from `ids` are already excluded automatically).

name: upsert_notes
description:
Create or update notes in bulk (1–100 per call). If a note object includes an `id`, the existing note is updated; if `id` is absent, a new note is created.

For new notes, `deck`, `note_type`, and `fields` are required. For updates, only `id` and the properties being changed are needed — omitted properties are left unchanged.

When a vector index is available, each result includes `neighbors`: the most similar existing notes ranked by cosine similarity, filtered to those above `neighbor_threshold` (default 0.5) and capped at `top_k_neighbors` (default 5). Use these for tag consistency (adopt tags from nearby notes), detecting near-duplicates (high scores suggest overlap), or understanding where a new note sits in the collection. Neighbors include note ID, similarity score, and tags — use list_notes or search_notes to inspect content if needed.

top_k_neighbors.description: Maximum number of similar-note neighbors to return per result. Default 5. Set to 0 to disable neighbor lookup.

neighbor_threshold.description: Minimum cosine similarity score for a neighbor to be included. Default 0.5. Higher values return only very similar notes.

id.description: Note ID. Present = update existing note, absent = create new note.

deck.description: Target deck (e.g., "Japanese::Vocabulary"). Required for new notes. On update, moves the note to this deck.

note_type.description: Note type (e.g., "Basic", "Cloze"). Required for new notes. Cannot be changed on update.

fields.description: Field key-value pairs matching the note type's field names (e.g., {"Front": "食べる", "Back": "to eat"}). Required for new notes. On update, only specified fields are modified; unspecified fields are unchanged.

tags.description: Tags for the note. On create, these are the note's tags. On update, replaces all existing tags — include any existing tags you want to keep.

notes.description: Array of note objects to create or update.

name: upsert_note_types
description:
Create or update note type definitions (1–10 per call). A note type defines the schema for notes: its fields, card templates (front/back HTML), and shared CSS styling.

If a note type object includes an `id`, the existing note type is updated; if `id` is absent, a new note type is created. For new note types, `name`, `fields`, `templates`, and `css` are required.

Card templates use Anki's replacement syntax: `{{FieldName}}` inserts a field value, `{{FrontSide}}` on the back template inserts the rendered front side. Cloze note types use `{{cloze:FieldName}}`.

Note: removing fields from an existing note type deletes that field's data from all notes of that type.

id.description: Note type ID. Present = update existing note type, absent = create new one.

name.description: Name for the note type (e.g., "Japanese Vocabulary"). Required for new note types.

fields.description: Ordered list of field names (e.g., ["Word", "Reading", "Meaning"]). Required for new note types. On update, replaces the full field list. Removing fields deletes that field's data from all existing notes of this type.

front.description: Front side HTML. Use {{FieldName}} to insert field values.

back.description: Back side HTML. Use {{FieldName}} for fields and {{FrontSide}} to insert the rendered front side.

templates.description: Card templates. Each template produces one card per note (except cloze types, where cards are generated from cloze deletions). Required for new note types.

css.description: CSS styling shared across all cards of this note type. Required for new note types.

is_cloze.description: If true, this is a cloze deletion note type. Templates should use {{cloze:FieldName}}. Default false. Cannot be changed on update.

note_types.description: Array of note type definitions to create or update.

name: delete_notes
description: Permanently delete notes and all their associated cards. This cannot be undone. Use list_notes or search_notes first to verify which notes will be deleted.

ids.description: Note IDs to delete. All cards belonging to these notes are also deleted.

name: delete_note_types
description: Delete note type definitions by ID. A note type can only be deleted if no notes currently use it. Check use counts via collection_info first.

ids.description: Note type IDs to delete. Fails for any note type that still has notes using it.
